response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished senior web and full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js credentials and broad experience across modern software stacks, but he fails to meet several hard requirements for this role. Most critically, there is no mention of Japanese language proficiency anywhere in the resume, which is listed as a non-negotiable requirement. His C/C++ experience is minimal and brief (one short stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015), his background has no embedded networking or Linux kernel work, and he has no demonstrated experience with security protocols such as MACsec, 802.1X, or Captive Portal. His profile is well-suited for a staff-level web or full-stack role, but it does not align with the embedded networking security domain this position requires.
strengths: Long, continuous software development career (15+ years) demonstrating broad technical adaptability across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded domains
Experience at high-caliber companies (Google, Meta) with large-scale, production-grade systems
Demonstrated low-level systems thinking in isolated contexts (D-language async I/O, C++ audio synthesis, NI LabWindows C components)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency mentioned — this is a stated hard requirement and its absence is disqualifying
No experience with embedded networking security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, MAC auth, Captive Portal, or LAN-switch firmware)
C programming is peripheral and short-lived in his history; primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js — the inverse of what this role demands

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
Minimal and dated C/Linux kernel experience
